title: Add & subtract polynomials: find the error
description: Choose which step an error was made in adding or subtracting polynomials.
topic_path: Module 1: Relationships between quantities and reasoning with equations and their graphs
Topic B: Lesson 8: Adding and subtracting polynomials with 2 variables
Add & subtract polynomials: find the error

assessment_items.question_content:
Isaac tried to subtract two polynomials. His work is shown below:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(4x^2+6xy-5y^2)-(x^2-3xy+9y^2)
\\\\
&=4x^2+6xy-5y^2-x^2+3xy-9y^2&\text{Step }1
\\\\
&=(4-1)x^2+(6+3)xy+(-5-9)y^2&\text{Step }2
\\\\
&=3x^2+9xy-14y^2&\text{Step }3
\end{align}$

**At which step did Isaac make an error, if at all?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
At $\text{Step }1$, Isaac got rid of the parentheses:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(4x^2+6xy-5y^2)-(x^2-3xy+9y^2)
\\\\
&=4x^2+6xy-5y^2-x^2+3xy-9y^2
\end{align}$

This step was done correctly, as he distributed the negative sign across all terms in the second trinomial.

At $\text{Step }2$, Isaac simplified the expression:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}4x^2+6xy-5y^2-x^2+3xy-9y^2
\\\\
&=(4-1)x^2+(6+3)xy+(-5-9)y^2
\end{align}$

This step was done correctly, as he only combined terms that are alike.

At $\text{Step }3$, Isaac simplified the coefficients of each term:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(4-1)x^2+(6+3)xy+(-5-9)y^2
\\\\
&=3x^2+9xy-14y^2
\end{align}$

This step was also done correctly.
In conclusion, Isaac's work is correct.

assessment_items.question_content:
Sanaz tried to subtract two polynomials. Her work is shown below:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(-3a^4-7a^2b^2+6b^4)-(2a^4-3a^2b^2+4b^4)
\\\\
&=-3a^4-7a^2b^2+6b^4-2a^4+3a^2b^2-4b^4&\text{Step }1
\\\\
&=(-3-7)a^6b^2+(6-2)a^4b^4+(3-4)a^2b^6&\text{Step }2
\\\\
&=-10a^6b^2+4a^4b^4-a^2b^6&\text{Step }3
\end{align}$

**At which step did Sanaz make an error, if at all?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
At $\text{Step }1$, Sanaz got rid of the parentheses:

$\begin{align}&(-3a^4-7a^2b^2+6b^4)-(2a^4-3a^2b^2+4b^4)\\
\\
=&-3a^4-7a^2b^2+6b^4-2a^4+3a^2b^2-4b^4
\end{align}$

This step was done correctly, as she distributed the negative sign across all terms in the second trinomial.

At $\text{Step }2$, Sanaz simplified the expression:

$\begin{align}&-3a^4-7a^2b^2+6b^4-2a^4+3a^2b^2-4b^4\\
\\
=&(-3-7)a^6b^2+(6-2)a^4b^4+(3-4)a^2b^6
\end{align}$

This step wasn't done correctly, as she combined terms that are not alike. For example, she combined $a^4$ terms with $a^2b^2$ terms by writing:

$-3a^4-7a^2b^2=(-3-7)a^6b^2$
In conclusion, Sanaz made a mistake at $\text{Step}\ 2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Victoria tried to subtract two polynomials. Her work is shown below:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(7a^2+4ab-b^2)-(2a^2-3ab+6b^2)
\\\\
&=7a^2+4ab-b^2-2a^2-3ab+6b^2&\text{Step }1
\\\\
&=(7-2)a^2+(4-3)ab+(-1+6)b^2&\text{Step }2
\\\\
&=5a^2+ab+5b^2&\text{Step }3
\end{align}$

**At which step did Victoria make an error, if at all?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
At $\text{Step }1$, Victoria got rid of the parentheses:

$\begin{align}&(7a^2+4ab-b^2)-(2a^2-3ab+6b^2)\\
\\
=&7a^2+4ab-b^2-2a^2-3ab+6b^2
\end{align}$

This step wasn't done correctly, as she only distributed the negative sign to the first term in the trinomial $2a^2-3ab+6b^2$, and not to the other terms.
In conclusion, Victoria made a mistake at $\text{Step }1$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Isatou tried to subtract two polynomials. Her work is shown below:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(3c^2-5cd+d^2)-(7c^2+4cd-9d^2)
\\\\
&=3c^2-5cd+d^2-7c^2+4cd-9d^2&\text{Step }1
\\\\
&=(3-7)c^2+(-5+4)cd+(1-9)d^2&\text{Step }2
\\\\
&=-4c^2-cd-8d^2&\text{Step }3
\end{align}$

**At which step did Isatou make an error, if at all?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
At $\text{Step }1$, Isatou got rid of the parentheses:

$\begin{align}&(3c^2-5cd+d^2)-(7c^2+4cd-9d^2)\\
\\
=&3c^2-5cd+d^2-7c^2+4cd-9d^2
\end{align}$

This step wasn't done correctly, as she only distributed the negative sign to the first term in the trinomial $7c^2+4cd-9d^2$, and not to the other terms.
In conclusion, Isatou made a mistake at $\text{Step }1$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Javier tried to subtract two polynomials. His work is shown below:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(x^4-3x^2y^2+y^4)-(-11x^4+4x^2y^2-3y^4)
\\\\
&=x^4-3x^2y^2+y^4+11x^4-4x^2y^2+3y^4&\text{Step }1
\\\\
&=(1-3)x^6+(-3+1)y^6 +(11-4)x^6+(-4+3)y^6&\text{Step }2
\\\\
&=-2x^6-2y^6+7x^6-y^6&\text{Step }3
\end{align}$

**At which step did Javier make an error, if at all?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
At $\text{Step }1$, Javier got rid of the parentheses:

$\begin{align}&(x^4-3x^2y^2+y^4)-(-11x^4+4x^2y^2-3y^4)\\
\\
=&x^4-3x^2y^2+y^4+11x^4-4x^2y^2+3y^4
\end{align}$

This step was done correctly, as he distributed the negative sign across all terms in the second trinomial.

At $\text{Step }2$, Javier simplified the expression:

$\begin{align}&x^4-3x^2y^2+y^4+11x^4-4x^2y^2+3y^4\\
\\
=&(1-3)x^6+(-3+1)y^6 +(11-4)x^6+(-4+3)y^6
\end{align}$

This step wasn't done correctly, as he combined terms that are not alike. For example, he combined $x^4$, $x^2y^2$, and $y^4$ terms by writing that:

$x^4-3x^2y^2+y^4=(1-3)x^6+(-3+1)y^6$
In conclusion, Javier made a mistake at $\text{Step }2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Shota tried to subtract two polynomials. His work is shown below:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(12a^2+3ab+4b^2)-(7a^2-5ab+b^2)
\\\\
&=12a^2+3ab+4b^2-7a^2+5ab-b^2&\text{Step }1
\\\\
&=(12+3)a^3+(3+4)b^3+(-7+5)a^3+(5-1)b^3&\text{Step }2
\\\\
&=15a^3+7b^3-2a^3+4b^3&\text{Step }3
\end{align}$

**At which step did Shota make an error, if at all?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
At $\text{Step }1$, Shota got rid of the parentheses:

$\begin{align}&(12a^2+3ab+4b^2)-(7a^2-5ab+b^2)\\
\\
=&12a^2+3ab+4b^2-7a^2+5ab-b^2
\end{align}$

This step was done correctly, as he distributed the negative sign across all terms in the second trinomial.

At $\text{Step }2$, Shota simplified the expression:

$\begin{align}&12a^2+3ab+4b^2-7a^2+5ab-b^2\\
\\
=&(12+3)a^3+(3+4)b^3+(-7+5)a^3+(5-1)b^3
\end{align}$

This step wasn't done correctly, as he combined terms that are not alike. For example, he combined $a^2$, $ab$, and $b^2$ terms by writing that:

$12a^2+3ab+4b^2=(12+3)a^3+(3+4)b^3$
In conclusion, Shota made a mistake at $\text{Step }2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Nikita tried to subtract two polynomials. Her work is shown below:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(3w^4-8w^2z^2+4z^4)-(5w^4+7w^2z^2-8z^4)
\\\\
&=3w^4-8w^2z^2+4z^4-5w^4-7w^2z^2+8z^4&\text{Step }1
\\\\
&=(3-8)w^6z^2+(4-5)w^4z^4+(-7+8)w^2z^6&\text{Step }2
\\\\
&=-5w^6z^2-w^4z^4+w^2z^6&\text{Step }3
\end{align}$

**At which step did Nikita make an error, if at all?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
At $\text{Step }1$, Nikita got rid of the parentheses:

$\begin{align}&(3w^4-8w^2z^2+4z^4)-(5w^4+7w^2z^2-8z^4)\\
\\
=&3w^4-8w^2z^2+4z^4-5w^4-7w^2z^2+8z^4
\end{align}$

This step was done correctly, as she distributed the negative sign across all terms in the second trinomial.

At $\text{Step }2$, Nikita simplified the expression:

$\begin{align}&3w^4-8w^2z^2+4z^4-5w^4-7w^2z^2+8z^4\\
\\
=&(3-8)w^6z^2+(4-5)w^4z^4+(-7+8)w^2z^6
\end{align}$

This step wasn't done correctly, as she combined terms that are not alike. For example, she combined $w^4$ terms with $w^2z^2$ terms by writing that:

$3w^4-8w^2z^2=(3-8)w^6z^2$
In conclusion, Nikita made a mistake at $\text{Step }2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Claire tried to subtract two polynomials. Her work is shown below:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(2x^2+2xy+5y^2)-(3x^2+4xy+6y^2)
\\\\
&=2x^2+2xy+5y^2-3x^2-4xy-6y^2&\text{Step }1
\\\\
&=(2+2)x^2y+(2+5)xy^2-(3+4)x^2y-(4+6)xy^2&\text{Step }2
\\\\
&=4x^2y+7xy^2-7x^2y-10xy^2&\text{Step }3
\end{align}$

**At which step did Claire make an error, if at all?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
At $\text{Step }1$, Claire got rid of the parentheses:

$\begin{align}&(2x^2+2xy+5y^2)-(3x^2+4xy+6y^2)\\
\\
=&2x^2+2xy+5y^2-3x^2-4xy-6y^2
\end{align}$

This step was done correctly, as she distributed the negative sign across all terms in the second trinomial.

At $\text{Step }2$, Claire simplified the expression:

$\begin{align}&2x^2+2xy+5y^2-3x^2-4xy-6y^2\\
\\
=&(2+2)x^2y+(2+5)xy^2-(3+4)x^2y-(4+6)xy^2
\end{align}$

This step wasn't done correctly, as she combined terms that are not alike. For example, she combined $x^2$, $xy$, and $y^2$ terms by writing that:

$2x^2+2xy+5y^2=(2+2)x^2y+(2+5)xy^2$
In conclusion, Claire made a mistake at $\text{Step }2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Brandon tried to subtract two polynomials. His work is shown below:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(7x^2-3x+8)-(3x^2+4x+5)
\\\\
&=7x^2-3x+8-3x^2-4x-5&\text{Step }1
\\\\
&=(7-3)x^2+(-3-4)x+(8-5)&\text{Step }2
\\\\
&=4x^2-7x+3&\text{Step }3
\end{align}$

**At which step did Brandon make an error, if at all?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
At $\text{Step }1$, Brandon got rid of the parentheses:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(7x^2-3x+8)-(3x^2+4x+5)
\\\\
&=7x^2-3x+8-3x^2-4x-5
\end{align}$

This step was done correctly, as he distributed the negative sign across all terms in the second trinomial.

At $\text{Step }2$, Brandon simplified the expression:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}7x^2-3x+8-3x^2-4x-5
\\\\
&=(7-3)x^2+(-3-4)x+(8-5)
\end{align}$

This step was done correctly, as he only combined terms that are alike.

At $\text{Step }3$, Brandon simplified the coefficients of each term:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(7-3)x^2+(-3-4)x+(8-5)
\\\\
&=4x^2-7x+3
\end{align}$

This step was also done correctly.
In conclusion, Brandon's work is correct.

assessment_items.question_content:
Nearlyse tried to subtract two polynomials. Her work is shown below:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(3a^2+7a-4)-(-2a^2+4a+9)
\\\\
&=3a^2+7a-4+2a^2-4a-9&\text{Step }1
\\\\
&=(3+2)a^2+(7-4)a+(-4-9)&\text{Step }2
\\\\
&=5a^2+3a-13&\text{Step }3
\end{align}$

**At which step did Nearlyse make an error, if at all?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
At $\text{Step }1$, Nearlyse got rid of the parentheses:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(3a^2+7a-4)-(-2a^2+4a+9)
\\\\
&=3a^2+7a-4+2a^2-4a-9
\end{align}$

This step was done correctly, as she distributed the negative sign across all terms in the second trinomial.

At $\text{Step }2$, Nearlyse simplified the expression:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}3a^2+7a-4+2a^2-4a-9
\\\\
&=(3+2)a^2+(7-4)a+(-4-9)
\end{align}$

This step was done correctly, as she only combined terms that are alike.

At $\text{Step }3$, Nearlyse simplified the coefficients of each term:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(3+2)a^2+(7-4)a+(-4-9)
\\\\
&=5a^2+3a-13
\end{align}$

This step was also done correctly.
In conclusion, Nearlyse's work is correct.

assessment_items.question_content:
Kenisha tried to subtract two polynomials. Her work is shown below:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(2b^2+3b+11)-(7b^2-5b+1)
\\\\
&=2b^2+3b+11-7b^2-5b+1&\text{Step }1
\\\\
&=(2-7)b^2+(3-5)b+(11+1)&\text{Step }2
\\\\
&=-5b^2-2b+12&\text{Step }3
\end{align}$

**At which step did Kenisha make an error, if at all?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
At $\text{Step }1$, Kenisha got rid of the parentheses:

$\begin{align}&(2b^2+3b+11)-(7b^2-5b+1)\\
\\
=&2b^2+3b+11-7b^2-5b+1
\end{align}$

This step wasn't done correctly, as she only distributed the negative sign to the first term in the trinomial $7b^2-5b+1$, and not to the other terms.
In conclusion, Kenisha made a mistake at $\text{Step }1$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Annie tried to subtract two polynomials. Her work is shown below:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(k^2+2k-13)-(-3k^2+9k+1)
\\\\
&=k^2+2k-13+3k^2+9k+1&\text{Step }1
\\\\
&=(1+3)k^2+(2+9)k+(-13+1)&\text{Step }2
\\\\
&=4k^2+11k-12&\text{Step }3
\end{align}$

**At which step did Annie make an error, if at all?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
At $\text{Step }1$, Annie got rid of the parentheses:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(k^2+2k-13)-(-3k^2+9k+1)
\\\\
&=k^2+2k-13+3k^2+9k+1
\end{align}$

This step wasn't done correctly, as she only distributed the negative sign to the first term in the trinomial $-3k^2+9k+1$, and not to the other terms.
In conclusion, Annie made a mistake at $\text{Step }1$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Mohamed tried to subtract two polynomials. His work is shown below:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(9y^2-4y-6)-(-3y^2+6y+2)
\\\\
&=9y^2-4y-6+3y^2+6y+2&\text{Step }1
\\\\
&=(9+3)y^2+(-4+6)y+(-6+2)&\text{Step }2
\\\\
&=12y^2+2y-4&\text{Step }3
\end{align}$

**At which step did Mohamed make an error, if at all?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
At $\text{Step }1$, Mohamed got rid of the parentheses:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(9y^2-4y-6)-(-3y^2+6y+2)
\\\\
&=9y^2-4y-6+3y^2+6y+2
\end{align}$

This step wasn't done correctly, as he only distributed the negative sign to the first term in the trinomial $-3y^2+6y+2$, and not to the other terms.
In conclusion, Mohamed made a mistake at $\text{Step }1$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Moussa tried to subtract two polynomials. His work is shown below:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(t^2+6t-3)-(7t^2-4t+1)
\\\\
&=t^2+6t-3-7t^2+4t-1&\text{Step }1
\\\\
&=(0-7)t^2+(6+4)t+(-3-1)&\text{Step }2
\\\\
&=-7t^2+10t-4&\text{Step }3
\end{align}$

**At which step did Moussa make an error, if at all?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
At $\text{Step }1$, Moussa got rid of the parentheses:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(t^2+6t-3)-(7t^2-4t+1)
\\\\
&=t^2+6t-3-7t^2+4t-1
\end{align}$

This step was done correctly, as he distributed the negative sign across all terms in the second trinomial.

At $\text{Step 2}$, Moussa simplified the expression:

$\begin{align}&t^2+6t-3-7t^2+4t-1\\
\\
=&(0-7)t^2+(6+4)t+(-3-1)
\end{align}$

This step wasn't done correctly, as he forgot that there is an implied coefficient of $1$ in front of the term $t^2$.
In conclusion, Moussa made a mistake at $\text{Step }2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Deniz tried to subtract two polynomials. Her work is shown below:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(3r^2-9r+3)-(-5r^2+7r-2)
\\\\
&=3r^2-9r+3+5r^2-7r+2&\text{Step }1
\\\\
&=(3-9)r^2+(5-7)r+(3+2)&\text{Step }2
\\\\
&=-6r^2-2r+5&\text{Step }3
\end{align}$

**At which step did Deniz make an error, if at all?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
At $\text{Step }1$, Deniz got rid of the parentheses:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(3r^2-9r+3)-(-5r^2+7r-2)
\\\\
&=3r^2-9r+3+5r^2-7r+2
\end{align}$

This step was done correctly, as she distributed the negative sign across all terms in the second trinomial.

At $\text{Step 2}$, Deniz simplified the expression:

$\begin{align}&3r^2-9r+3+5r^2-7r+2
\\\\
=&(3-9)r^2+(5-7)r+(3+2)
\end{align}$

This step wasn't done correctly, as she combined terms that are not alike. For example, she combined $r^2$ terms with $r$ terms by writing that:

$3r^2-9r=(3-9)r^2$
In conclusion, Deniz made a mistake at $\text{Step }2$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Fatu tried to subtract two polynomials. Her work is shown below:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(2p^2-3p-7)-(3p^2+p-5)
\\\\
&=2p^2-3p-7-3p^2-p+5&\text{Step }1
\\\\
&=(2-3)p^2+(-3-1)p+(-7+5)&\text{Step }2
\\\\
&=p^2-2p+2&\text{Step }3
\end{align}$

**At which step did Fatu make an error, if at all?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
At $\text{Step }1$, Fatu got rid of the parentheses:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}(2p^2-3p-7)-(3p^2+p-5)
\\\\
&=2p^2-3p-7-3p^2-p+5
\end{align}$

This step was done correctly, as she distributed the negative sign across all terms in the second trinomial.

At $\text{Step }2$, Fatu simplified the expression:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}2p^2-3p-7-3p^2-p+5
\\\\
&=(2-3)p^2+(-3-1)p+(-7+5)
\end{align}$

This step was done correctly, as she only combined terms that are alike.

At $\text{Step }3$, Fatu simplified the coefficients of each term:

$\begin{align}
&(2-3)p^2+(-3-1)p+(-7+5)
\\\\
=&p^2-2p+2
\end{align}$

This step wasn't done correctly, as she made three arithmetic errors in adding and subtracting signed numbers.
In conclusion, Fatu made a mistake at $\text{Step }3$.
